assertions.description: Symbol.asyncDispose is exposed as a well-known symbol for async disposable resources.
AsyncDisposableStack is exposed as a global constructor.
AsyncDisposableStack instances expose a use() method that registers an async disposable.
AsyncDisposableStack instances expose disposeAsync(), the async counterpart of dispose().

assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=new AsyncDisposableStack();const p=s.disposeAsync();return p && typeof p.then==='function';}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: AsyncDisposableStack instances expose defer() for ad-hoc async cleanup callbacks.
The await using declaration syntax is accepted inside async functions.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{new Function('return (async()=>{ await using r = { [Symbol.asyncDispose]: async()=>{} }; })();');return true;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: Multiple await using resources dispose in last-in-first-out order.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const order=[];const fn=new Function('order','return (async()=>{ await using first = { [Symbol.asyncDispose]: async()=>order.push("first") }; await using second = { [Symbol.asyncDispose]: async()=>order.push("second") }; })();');return fn(order).then(()=>order.join(',')==='second,first');}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: When the body and async disposer both throw, the thrown value is a SuppressedError with error and suppressed entries.
assertions.test: (async()=>{try{const fn=new Function('return (async()=>{ await using r = { [Symbol.asyncDispose]: async()=>{ throw new Error("dispose"); } }; throw new Error("body"); })();');await fn();return false;}catch(e){return e.name==='SuppressedError'&&e.error?.message==='dispose'&&e.suppressed?.message==='body';}})()
